overview.description: AI-powered internal communications platform and earned wage access for employers
overview.description_long: PalmApp Tech builds two products for employers: PalmApp Smart Workplace, an AI platform that personalizes corporate communications into videos, podcasts and HR stories for employees, with a real-time voice assistant for HR queries; and PalmApp EWA (Zálohy), an earned wage access service letting employees draw a portion of their salary before payday, with a 2% cashback debit card. Available in Czech, English, German and Italian. EWA service operates in Czech Republic and Slovakia.

history.origin_story: Founded in Prague in November 2020 as PalmApp Tech s.r.o., building AI tools to simplify internal corporate communications and HR processes.

key_milestones.event: Founded as PalmApp Tech s.r.o. in Prague (registered 27 Nov 2020)
Launched PalmApp EWA (earned wage access) for Czech and Slovak markets
Expanded Smart Workplace to multilingual support (CZ, EN, DE, IT)

risk_assessment.agi_displacement_rationale: The Smart Workplace product (personalized AI videos, podcasts, voice assistant for HR) is a thin application layer over generative AI. Larger HR platforms (SAP SuccessFactors, Workday, Microsoft Viva) are rapidly adding identical capabilities, and foundation models make this easy to replicate. The EWA product provides a partial moat through fintech regulation and payment infrastructure.
risk_assessment.market_risk_rationale: Earned wage access is a real and growing market in CEE, with proven demand. AI employee communications is also a genuine pain point. However, both segments face strong competition from larger incumbent HR vendors with existing enterprise relationships.
risk_assessment.funding_risk_rationale: No external funding rounds identified. Company has only 9 employees, was founded in 2020, and there is no public evidence of revenue or investor backing. Runway and sustainability are uncertain.
risk_assessment.overall_risk_rationale: Very small bootstrapped team with a product in a highly competitive space (AI communications) that is easily commoditized by foundation models. The EWA fintech product offers some differentiation but requires regulatory approval and capital. Without external funding or a clear moat, the company faces significant risk.

metadata.notes: Small Prague-based startup (IČO: 09707123, founded 2020-11-27). Office at Impact Hub Praha 5 Smíchov. No CNAIP page found. LinkedIn shows 9 employees, 741 followers. No Glassdoor presence found. Products: Smart Workplace (AI HR comms) and EWA/Zálohy (earned wage access, CZ+SK). Business model likely SaaS subscription for Smart Workplace + transaction fees for EWA.
